title: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
summary: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
extracted_text: Russia conducted a massive strike using long-range precision air, land, and sea-based weapons and attack drones. Targets included military-industrial facilities, fuel and energy complexes in Kyiv and the Kyiv region, and military airfields. Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko reported 10 deaths and 34 injuries. Poland scrambled fighter jets and raised air defense readiness. Finland imposed a temporary aviation restriction zone in the eastern Gulf.

title: Google loses legal fight over 4.1 billion-euro EU antitrust fine
summary: Google loses legal fight over 4.1 billion-euro EU antitrust fine
extracted_text: The European Commission's top court has upheld Google's fine of around 4.1 billion euros ($4.67 billion) over alleged anti-competitive practices. In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance to give unfair advantage to its own apps via pre-installation deals with smartphone makers.

title: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
summary: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
extracted_text: Kospi index closed down 7.89%. Samsung Electronics declined 9.06% to 286,000 won. SK Hynix fell 14.57% to 2,187,000 won. The rout followed a broad US semiconductor selloff with Micron Technology -10%+ and Sandisk -10%+. SK Hynix CEO announced a 100 trillion won ($64.37 billion) investment in South Korea for AI memory and packaging.

title: OpenAI proposes U.S. government own 5% stake to address political blowback
summary: OpenAI proposes U.S. government own 5% stake to address political blowback
extracted_text: OpenAI has proposed giving the U.S. government a 5% ownership stake in the company, worth approximately $42.6 billion based on OpenAI's March 2026 post-money valuation of $852 billion. Sam Altman reportedly argued it's the best way to share the upside of AI with the public. Altman suggested Washington hold 5% of each leading U.S. AI developer via a government vehicle.

title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh at ECB Forum: No urgency to raise rates; inflation expectations easing
summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh at ECB Forum: No urgency to raise rates; inflation expectations easing
extracted_text: Warsh: 'We're all in the price stability business. We've seen that prices are too high.' Core PCE at 3.4% in May, headline PCE at 4.1%. Warsh declined to signal July rate decision but said 'If there were people who thought this central bank was going to be comfortable with an inflation objective above 2%, they'd be disappointed.' Markets price >60% chance of a Fed rate hike in September. ADP private payrolls June: +98,000 vs 110,000 expected.

title: Crude oil falls below $68 as Strait of Hormuz shipments increase, US-Iran talks progress
summary: Crude oil falls below $68 as Strait of Hormuz shipments increase, US-Iran talks progress
extracted_text: WTI crude oil: $67.93 (-0.95%), Brent: $70.92 (-0.90%). Oil fell to lowest since late February as Strait of Hormuz shipments increased. US official said crude flows exceeded 10 million barrels/day. Iranian exports surged past 40 million barrels after US naval blockade lifted. Russian exports hit record levels. WTI posted steepest quarterly decline since 2020.

title: Gold strengthens above $4,000 as Fed's Warsh signals no urgency to hike
summary: Gold strengthens above $4,000 as Fed's Warsh signals no urgency to hike
extracted_text: Gold: $4,076.15 (+1.11%), +44.25 on the day. Previous: $4,031.36. Warsh stated inflation expectations had eased, suggesting no urgency to raise rates. Markets pricing >60% chance of a September hike. Gold earlier slipped below $4,000 after strong JOLTS data.

title: US Dollar Index holds steady around 101 ahead of June jobs report
summary: US Dollar Index holds steady around 101 ahead of June jobs report
extracted_text: DXY: 101.0214 (-0.37%). Dollar eased from 15-month high of 101.6 after Warsh said inflationary risks are lower. ADP indicated 98,000 jobs added in June, slightly under expectations. Cross rates: EUR/USD 1.142, GBP/USD 1.336, USD/JPY 161.13 (-0.88%).

title: Treasury yields: 10Y at 4.50%, markets await NFP; 2Y-10Y spread narrow
summary: Treasury yields: 10Y at 4.50%, markets await NFP; 2Y-10Y spread narrow
extracted_text: US 10Y: 4.50% (+0.013%), 2Y: 4.19% (+0.002%), 5Y: 4.25% (+0.013%), 30Y: 4.99% (+0.013%). Warsh's push to shrink Fed's Treasury holdings lifted yields. ADP data: 98,000 private jobs, slightly below estimates. Rate traders see one December hike; some expect multiple.

title: Premarket Stock Trading Data: Dow, S&P, NASDAQ Futures - CNBC
summary: Premarket Stock Trading Data: Dow, S&P, NASDAQ Futures
extracted_text: Dow Future: 52,694, Implied Open +133.76. S&P 500 Future: 7,539, Implied Open +4.77. Nasdaq Future: 29,958.75, Implied Open -55.38. Russell Future: 3,034.3, Implied Open -7.29. VIX: 16.73 (+0.84%). Asia: Hang Seng +0.76%, Nikkei -2.47%, Shanghai -2.03%. Europe: STOXX50 +0.33%, DAX +0.32%, FTSE +0.53%.

title: Employment Situation Summary - June 2026 scheduled for release July 2 at 8:30 AM ET
extracted_text: The Employment Situation for June is scheduled to be released on Thursday, July 2, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. (EDT). Prior report (May 2026): nonfarm payrolls +125,000, unemployment rate 4.3%. Consensus for June: nonfarm payrolls +115,000.

title: Asia-Pacific and European Markets Summary - July 2, 2026
Sector Performance: Financials lead, Technology lags sharply - July 1 close
summary: Sector Performance: Financials lead, Technology lags sharply
extracted_text: US Sectors July 1 close: Financials 911.85 (+2.13%), Energy 806.45 (-0.56%), Consumer Discretionary 1,922.81 (+0.82%), Technology 6,663.53 (-1.84%), Consumer Staples 919.95 (-0.33%). DJ Transports +0.97%, Utilities -0.92%. Nasdaq 100 -1.54% worst index performer.

title: US 10 Year Treasury Yield holds at 4.50% with curve dynamics; NFP in focus
summary: US 10 Year Treasury Yield holds at 4.50% with curve dynamics; NFP in focus
extracted_text: US Treasury yields July 2: 3-MO 3.816%, 2-YR 4.176%, 5-YR 4.248%, 10-YR 4.495%, 30-YR 4.987%. Curve positively sloped: 10Y-2Y at +32bp. Fed funds rate at 3.75%. Inflation Rate 4.20% (May), Unemployment 4.30% (May).

title: ADP private payrolls June 2026: +98,000, below expectations of 110,000
summary: ADP private payrolls June 2026: +98,000, below expectations of 110,000
extracted_text: ADP private payrolls (June): +98,000 vs. +122,000 prior and 110,000 expected. Ahead of official nonfarm payrolls (expected +115,000, unemployment rate 4.3%). The data showed low firing rates but slower hiring.

title: Stocks Moving in Premarket Today (July 2, 2026)
extracted_text: Pre-market mover data not yet populated (data platform still warming up). Prior session notable movers: FMC +8.3%, General Mills (GIS) +4.4%, ServiceNow (NOW) +4.1%. Losers: Micron (MU) -10%+, Sandisk (SNDK) -10%+, Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) -1% to -2%.

title: S&P 500, Dow rise to start Q3; Nasdaq falls on chip weakness - July 1 recap
summary: S&P 500, Dow rise to start Q3; Nasdaq falls on chip weakness
extracted_text: FMC surged 8.3% in premarket, General Mills (GIS) rose 4.4%, ServiceNow (NOW) gained 4.1%. Essex Property Trust (ESS) tumbled 11.6%, Public Storage (PSA) fell 4.1%, Universal Health Services (UHS) dropped 4%.

title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing amid record heatwave, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
summary: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing amid record heatwave, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
extracted_text: Amid a record heatwave, EU faces tension between trade policy and reliance on Chinese cooling imports. Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing but faces practical constraints.

title: Citi UK CEO: M&A is 'on fire' as large-cap firms simplify
summary: Citi UK CEO: M&A is 'on fire' as large-cap firms simplify
extracted_text: Citi U.K. CEO Tiina Lee: M&A is 'on fire' as large-cap firms simplify. Suggests robust dealmaking pipeline continuing into second half of 2026.
